overview: Radiant-MVT is an AI-assisted trading intelligence platform for commodity trading teams. It brings decision management, live risk, market analysis, vessel operations, communications, compliance, AI tooling, and executive visibility into one workspace.
chat_capabilities: Explain what each screen does and where a feature lives.
Navigate directly to the correct page when a request is explicit.

Run supported in-app tasks such as generating briefings, refreshing market data, applying curve shifts, opening emails, sending drafted replies, testing connectors, switching AI providers, and launching scenarios.
Use the currently selected decision, email, connector, or alert when a request depends on a specific record.

screens.title: Decision Queue
screens.summary: Your prioritised list of decisions for the day, ordered by urgency, deadline, and potential impact.
features: Urgency-ranked decision cards with countdowns and estimated impact.
AI Decision Briefing panel for a market-aware summary of what matters most.
Decision reasoning modal that explains why a recommendation matters.
Quick actions such as review, snooze, and follow-up.
tasks: Open a specific decision and review the reasoning.
Complete or snooze a decision item.
chat_examples: What is the most urgent decision right now?

sections.title: What you see
items: A greeting bar with live date, time, and key decision KPIs.
Decision cards grouped by urgency with deadlines and impact.
An AI briefing area for a narrative morning summary.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Start with the top critical card and review the reasoning if context is needed.
Use the primary action to open the next step in your workflow.
Snooze non-urgent items so the queue reflects real priorities.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising the queue into a short trader briefing.
Explaining why a decision has been prioritised.
Opening the relevant area of the app for the selected decision.

screens.title: Trader Dashboard
screens.summary: A live overview of desk performance, current exposures, alerts, news, and activity in one place.
features: Role-aware KPI strip for P&L, VaR, decisions, and alerts.
Customisable tile layout with drag-and-drop ordering.
Book P&L, intraday chart, heat maps, alerts, news, and blotter tiles.
Fast drill-down into current trading hotspots.
tasks: Inspect alerts, blotter activity, or heat map hotspots.
chat_examples: Show me my top risk from the dashboard.

sections.title: What you see
items: Headline desk KPIs at the top of the page.
A grid of tiles covering P&L, alerts, heat maps, news, and blotter.
Layout controls so each user can tune their workspace.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Use this page as the first stop after login to understand the desk state.
Rearrange tiles so the most important views stay above the fold.
Drill into Positions, AI Intelligence, or Market Data when something stands out.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining unusual P&L moves.
Summarising the most important alerts and news.
Pointing you to the next page to investigate.

screens.title: Positions & Risk
screens.summary: Detailed position, hedge, exposure, and VaR analysis across the desk.
features: Physical, financial, and all-position filters.
Position grid with exposure, mark, P&L, hedge ratio, and VaR.
Exposure-by-tenor, counterparty, and forward curve views.
VaR explainer with stressed VaR and board-limit utilisation.
tasks: Review hedge ratios and commodity VaR.
chat_examples: Take me to the Brent curve.

sections.title: What you see
items: A detailed book-level view of open positions and risk metrics.
Supporting charts that explain how risk is distributed.
Forward-curve context to connect position risk with market structure.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Filter between physical and financial exposures to narrow the book.
Use the grid to identify outsized risk, weak hedges, or large VaR contributors.
Move to Market Data or AI Intelligence when you need scenario or hedge analysis.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Highlighting the biggest exposure on the screen.
Explaining risk concentration in plain English.
Sending you to the next page for action.

screens.title: AI Intelligence Centre
screens.summary: The desk's AI workspace for hedge advice, trade ideas, anomalies, forecasts, and pre-mortems.
features: Hedge Advisor with factor attribution and recommended structures.
Trade idea scanner and anomaly alert summary panels.
Pre-mortem workflow for testing the current book against failure scenarios.
Forecast narrative generation for supported commodities.
tasks: Run Hedge Advisor for a selected commodity.
Run the pre-mortem on the current book.
chat_examples: Run a pre-mortem on my book.
Generate a forecast narrative for Brent.

sections.title: What you see
items: AI modules focused on recommendation, anomaly detection, and explanation.
Commodity selectors and action buttons that trigger specific analyses.
Narrative panels that turn market and position context into guidance.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Select the relevant commodity or book area before running an analysis.
Use Hedge Advisor when exposure is known and you want a response structure.
Use Pre-mortem when you want to stress a current idea before acting.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Running the supported modules directly from chat.
Explaining output in trader-friendly language.
Connecting AI findings to the relevant screen for execution.

screens.title: Performance & Analytics
screens.summary: Performance tracking against plan, including run rate, attribution, and missed-opportunity context.
features: YTD P&L, target attainment, and forecast metrics.
Monthly charts and waterfall-style attribution.
Opportunity-cost and shortfall investigation hooks.
Desk-level performance comparison views.

sections.title: What you see
items: Summary KPIs for current progress versus plan.
Trend and attribution views for performance analysis.
Signals that link underperformance to behaviour or market conditions.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Start with target attainment and forecast to judge current trajectory.
Use attribution to understand what drove gains or shortfalls.
Move into Decision Intelligence when you need forensic explanation.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising why the desk is ahead or behind plan.
Explaining the biggest contributors to performance.
Guiding you toward missed-opportunity analysis.

screens.title: Decision Intelligence
screens.summary: Forensic review of missed decisions and institutional memory search for similar situations.
features: AI forensics workflow for reviewing what the desk missed.
Desk Brain search for prior trade patterns and comparable decisions.
Narrative explanation of decision quality and missed value.
tasks: Search Desk Brain for similar trades.
chat_examples: What did we miss last quarter?
Search Desk Brain for Urals arb history.

sections.title: What you see
items: A forensics area focused on missed opportunities and decision timing.
A Desk Brain search tool for institutional memory retrieval.
AI narrative output that explains patterns, not just numbers.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Run forensics when you want a structured post-mortem of desk behaviour.
Use Desk Brain when you need precedent or pattern recall.
Cross-reference with Performance and Boardroom for management-level storytelling.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Launching forensics from chat.
Searching historical structures through Desk Brain.
Summarising why an opportunity was missed.

screens.title: Market Data & Curves
screens.summary: Live market prices, curve structure, spreads, and scenario testing for key commodities.
features: Live prices and spread panels with refresh controls.
Forward curves for Brent, WTI, Ethane, Naphtha, and EUA.
Natural-language curve shifter for scenario testing.
Market headlines linked to the active commodity.
chat_examples: Refresh market data and open the Brent curve.
Shift Brent up $3 and show the impact.

sections.title: What you see
items: Current pricing and spread context for major traded markets.
A forward-curve chart with quick curve selectors.
A scenario input for natural-language market shocks.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Pick a curve and review current shape before running scenarios.
Use the scenario box to describe a price or structure move in plain language.
Compare the output with Positions & Risk when testing book sensitivity.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Navigating to the correct commodity curve.
Applying supported curve shifts for you.
Explaining current market moves and related headlines.

screens.title: Vessels & Logistics
screens.summary: Operational view of fleet activity, cargo timing, route status, and voyage economics.
features: Vessel cards with route and timing context.
Interactive map and delay visibility.
Voyage economics views and cargo pipeline awareness.
Operational context tied back to trading impact.
tasks: Inspect vessel route and voyage economics.
Review delay impact on trading decisions.
Monitor cargo timing and vessel status.
chat_examples: Will the Innovation delay affect my hedge?
Show me voyage economics for Hermod.
Explain the impact of current vessel delays.

sections.title: What you see
items: Fleet status and route visibility for operationally sensitive cargoes.
Map-based tracking that highlights delays and route context.
Voyage economics information for commercially relevant review.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Check this page whenever vessel timing could change exposure or execution.
Focus on delay signals and then review linked operational impact.
Move to Communications or Positions if a delay requires an action.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining whether a delay matters to the desk.
Summarising voyage economics in plain language.
Guiding you to the next operational or trading step.

screens.title: Communications Hub
screens.summary: Action-oriented inbox with AI summaries, linked trade context, and drafted responses.
features: Priority inbox with urgency filters.
Message detail panel with AI analysis and linked business context.
Drafted reply workflow for supported communications.
Mark-actioned flow for operational follow-up.
tasks: Send the drafted reply for the selected email.

sections.title: What you see
items: A prioritised inbox rather than a generic mail view.
AI-generated message context to reduce reading time.
A detail panel with trade, vessel, or operational context where available.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Start with critical and high-priority items first.
Open a message to review the AI summary and draft response.
Use mark-actioned after the operational step has been completed.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Opening a specific email for you.
Sending a prepared reply on supported records.
Summarising the inbox into an action list.

screens.title: Compliance & Audit
screens.summary: Regulatory status, filing awareness, and audit trail visibility for desk operations.
features: Regulatory filing status and upcoming deadlines.
Audit trail for platform and AI actions.
Desk-level compliance review support and controls.
chat_examples: What is my next filing deadline?

sections.title: What you see
items: A compact operational view of regulatory obligations and evidence trails.
Audit information that supports accountability for human and AI actions.
Deadline visibility for time-sensitive reporting.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Use this page for confidence checks before or after sensitive actions.
Review the audit trail when you need to confirm who did what and when.
Follow upcoming deadlines proactively rather than waiting for escalation.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Summarising current compliance posture.
Pointing you to the next filing or review item.
Explaining how audit information is organised.

screens.title: External Systems Configuration
screens.summary: Control centre for connectors that power feeds, AI providers, and external trading systems.
features: Connector dashboard grouped by source type.
Create, update, test, and remove connector records.
Provider-aware status and connectivity testing.
Credential and integration management workflow.
chat_examples: Where do I update API keys?

sections.title: What you see
items: A list of integrations grouped by functional category.
Health and status visibility for connectors that drive the platform.
Action buttons for test, edit, and lifecycle management.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Open this page whenever feeds or AI providers need setup or validation.
Use test actions after updating credentials or endpoints.
Keep connector naming clear so AI and users can reference them consistently.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Taking you straight to Configuration from chat.
Testing a supported connector for you.
Explaining which connector powers which capability.

screens.title: AI Studio
screens.summary: The enterprise control plane for AI prompts, agent definitions, tool permissions, and user context.
features: Agent list with default-chat selection and version history.
Prompt templates, tool scope, screen scope, and model settings.
Safe test harness for prompt and agent output.
Persistent user AI profile editing.
tasks: Inspect the current chat copilot configuration.
Edit or save an agent definition.
Run a safe test against a selected screen context.
chat_examples: What context does Chat Copilot see?
How should I tune this prompt safely?

sections.title: What you see
items: A workspace for managing AI agents instead of using hidden config files.
Version and setting visibility for prompts, models, and tool permissions.
A test area for validating changes before broader use.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Inspect the default chat agent before changing prompts or permissions.
Use the test harness after every meaningful agent change.
Update the user profile to improve how AI responds across the app.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Explaining what the current chat agent can access.
Guiding you to the relevant AI Studio control.
Helping you validate whether a prompt change is safe.

screens.title: Help Center
screens.summary: Built-in help workspace that explains every screen, workflow, and supported AI action inside the app.
features: Two-panel layout with grouped navigation on the left and detailed guidance on the right.
Feature, workflow, and chat prompt documentation for each area of the app.
Direct links into the relevant screen.
AI-assisted task buttons for supported actions.
tasks: Browse grouped documentation without leaving the app.
Open a linked screen from the guide.
Ask Radiant AI to explain or perform a supported task.
chat_examples: How do I use the Decision Queue?
Show me where connectors are configured.

sections.title: What you see
items: A navigation tree that groups the platform by workflow area.
A detailed content panel with features, tasks, and AI prompts.
Quick actions to open the destination screen or continue in chat.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Pick a workflow area on the left to explore the related screens.
Use the detail panel to understand what the screen does before navigating.
Trigger the AI task buttons when you want help or automation.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Answering questions about any screen in the guide.
Navigating you to the page you need.
Performing supported in-app actions directly from chat.

screens.title: Boardroom View
screens.summary: Executive summary view of desk performance, strategic upside, and capital efficiency.
features: Top-quartile gap analysis and strategic performance context.
Executive-friendly summaries for cross-book comparison.
Capital-efficiency and uplift framing for management review.
tasks: Compare desk or book performance at a higher level.
chat_examples: What is the top-quartile uplift potential?

sections.title: What you see
items: A management-facing version of desk performance and opportunity context.
Simplified visuals designed for strategic review rather than execution.
Uplift framing to support planning and board conversations.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Use this page when the audience is executive rather than trading.
Start with uplift and gap analysis before drilling into source screens.
Move to Performance or Decision Intelligence for supporting detail.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Producing a concise management summary.
Explaining uplift potential and performance gaps.
Pointing you to the supporting operational detail.

screens.title: Admin / Demo Control
screens.summary: Operational control area for scenarios, system health, and administrative shortcuts.
features: Scenario launcher for demo and incident-style cases.
AI configuration and health shortcuts.
System status visibility for supporting services.
tasks: Review AI connectivity and demo controls.

sections.title: What you see
items: Operational buttons designed for support, demo, and administrative flows.
System checks that confirm whether core services are healthy.
Scenario shortcuts for presenting the product or simulating incidents.

sections.title: How to use it
items: Use this page for environment confidence checks and demo control.
Trigger scenarios carefully because they change what other screens show.
Review service health before debugging missing AI or data behaviour.

sections.title: AI can help with
items: Launching supported scenarios for you.
Guiding you to the right admin control.
Explaining which environment issue to check next.
